content_type: vintage Americana illustration (shared as messaging image)
genre_refs: Mid-century American commercial illustration / Norman Rockwell-style nostalgia; 'A Church Picnic' by Arthur Sarnoff
description: A reproduced vintage mid-20th-century painted illustration titled 'A Church Picnic' by Arthur Sarnoff, posted by the U.S. Department of Labor. The colorful, idealized illustration depicts an idyllic 1950s-era white American community gathering on a sunny day: a white clapboard church with a steeple in the background, vintage cars (yellow station wagon, blue and cream sedans) parked along a lawn, families in period clothing gathered around picnic tables laden with food, a woman in a pink dress serving, children playing, a man carrying picnic baskets, a young couple sitting on rocks by a stream dipping their feet, a little girl in a yellow dress wading, and a dog. The artist's signature appears at lower left. There is no on-image slogan text; the messaging is supplied by the tweet caption: '250 Years of One Nation Under God. Happy Sunday, America. ‘A Church Picnic’ —Arthur Sarnoff'. The propaganda technique is nostalgic Americana evoking a homogeneous, faith-and-family vision of national identity tied to the country's 250th-anniversary (semiquincentennial) framing. Marked is_designed=true because it is a deliberately curated/branded messaging image, though the artwork itself is a pre-existing painting rather than an agency-made graphic.
notable_text: (no on-image text) — caption: '250 Years of One Nation Under God. Happy Sunday, America. ‘A Church Picnic’ —Arthur Sarnoff'
